reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully encodes the Putnam problem. `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℤ` is an n×n matrix with integer entries. `(A r) ⬝ᵥ (A r)` is the dot product of row r with itself, and `Even`/`Odd` match the parity conditions. `Pairwise fun r1 r2 ↦ Odd ((A r1) ⬝ᵥ (A r2))` unfolds to `∀ r1 r2, r1 ≠ r2 → Odd ((A r1) ⬝ᵥ (A r2))`, exactly "every dot product of two different rows is odd".
